member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.IHttpClientFactory
member.summary: Factory for creating and configuring HttpClient instances with consistent settings. Provides typed HTTP clients for different integration scenarios with proper timeouts and retry policies.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.DefaultHttpClientFactory
member.summary: Default implementation of HTTP client factory using standard HttpClient.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.DefaultHttpClientFactory.CreateWebhookClient
member.summary: Creates an HttpClient configured for webhook delivery. Per-attempt timeout, retry and circuit breaker behavior are applied by in the pipeline, so the client-level timeout is left unbounded to avoid double-timing-out a request that is still within its retry budget.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.HttpClientConfiguration
member.summary: Configuration for HTTP client factory setup during dependency injection.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions
member.summary: Tunable knobs for the webhook delivery resilience pipeline (timeout, retry, circuit breaker).

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions.RequestTimeout
member.summary: Maximum time allowed for a single delivery attempt. Defaults to 5 seconds.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions.MaxRetryAttempts
member.summary: Number of retries performed after the initial attempt on transient failures. Defaults to 2.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions.RetryBaseDelay
member.summary: Base delay used for the exponential-with-jitter backoff between retries. Defaults to 200ms.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions.FailureThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive failed attempts (post-retry) required to trip the circuit for a host. Defaults to 5.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceOptions.OpenDuration
member.summary: How long the circuit stays open for a host before a single probe request is allowed through. Defaults to 30 seconds.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitPhase
member.summary: Phase of a single host's circuit breaker.

member.name: F:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitPhase.Closed
member.summary: Requests flow normally; failures are being counted.

member.name: F:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitPhase.Open
member.summary: Requests are rejected without being sent to the remote endpoint.

member.name: F:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitPhase.HalfOpen
member.summary: The open duration has elapsed; a single probe request is allowed to decide whether to close or reopen.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookHostCircuit
member.summary: Mutable per-host circuit breaker state. Thread-safe via an internal lock.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookHostCircuit.Phase
member.summary: Current phase of the circuit.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookHostCircuit.TryAcquire(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether a request is currently allowed to proceed to the remote endpoint. Transitions to once the open duration has elapsed, admitting exactly one probe request.
member.param: Configured duration a trip stays open before probing.
member.returns: if the request may proceed; otherwise .

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookHostCircuit.RecordSuccess
member.summary: Records a successful delivery attempt, closing the circuit and resetting the failure count.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookHostCircuit.RecordFailure(System.Int32)
member.summary: Records a failed delivery attempt. Trips the circuit open once consecutive failures have been observed, or immediately re-opens it if the failure occurred during a half-open probe.
member.param: Number of consecutive failures required to trip the circuit.
member.returns: if this failure just tripped the circuit open.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitBreakerRegistry
member.summary: Registry of per-host circuit breaker state for webhook deliveries. Registered as a singleton so state survives across the transient instances created per outgoing handler.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitBreakerRegistry.GetCircuit(System.String)
member.summary: Gets (creating if absent) the circuit breaker state for the given host.
member.param: Destination host of the webhook endpoint.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or empty.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitOpenException
member.summary: Thrown when a webhook delivery is rejected because the circuit breaker for the destination host is open.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitOpenException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: The host whose circuit is currently open.

member.name: P:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookCircuitOpenException.Host
member.summary: The destination host whose circuit rejected the request.

member.name: T:FeatureFlags.Integration.WebhookResilienceHandler
member.summary: Delegating handler that applies a per-request timeout, jittered retry on 5xx/408 responses and transient transport failures, and a per-host circuit breaker to outgoing webhook delivery requests. When the circuit trips open, a is published on the event bus so the dead-endpoint condition shows up in the audit trail instead of silently backing up dispatch.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.exception: Thrown when any dependency is null.

member.exception: Thrown when the destination host's circuit breaker is open.

member.summary: Validates the user context has required identity information.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Models.UserContext.GetConsistentHash(System.String)
member.summary:
Generates a stable hash for consistent percentage-based rollout evaluation. Uses userId as the stable identifier for rollout bucketing.
Algorithm: SHA-256 hash of "{canonicalUserId}:{featureFlagKey}" where canonicalUserId is the numeric UserId converted to string if it's numeric, otherwise the raw UserId. The first 4 bytes of the hash are converted to a uint and used for bucket calculation. This ensures: - Same input always produces same output (deterministic across app restarts) - Uniform distribution across buckets (cryptographic hash properties) - No collisions for different inputs (extremely low probability)
member.param: The feature flag key for rollout bucketing
member.returns: Bucket number between 0 and 99 inclusive

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.ComputeSha256(System.String)
member.summary: Computes SHA-256 hash of input string and returns as hex-encoded lowercase string. Used for consistent hashing in rollout bucketing and data integrity verification.

member.summary: Computes SHA-512 hash for additional security in sensitive operations.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.ComputeHashBucket(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts SHA-256 hash to a numeric value (0-99) for percentage rollout bucketing. Ensures same input always produces same output for consistent user experiences.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.ComputeMd5(System.String)
member.summary: Computes MD5 hash (useful for ETags and quick checksums, not for security). Warning: MD5 is cryptographically weak, use SHA-256 for security-sensitive operations.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.ComputeFnv1aHash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes 32-bit FNV-1a hash for faster but less collision-resistant hashing. Suitable for hash tables and quick lookups, not cryptographic use.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.HashPassword(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Hashes password using PBKDF2 with SHA-256, suitable for user credentials. Uses random salt to prevent rainbow table attacks.

member.summary: Verifies a password against a PBKDF2 hash created by HashPassword.

member.summary: Generates a secure random hash suitable for tokens or session IDs.

member.name: M:FeatureFlags.Utilities.HashingUtilities.ComputeHmacSha256(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature for the given payload using the provided secret.
